author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, Mark viii. 34, ‘If any man would come after me, let him deny himself,’ etc. And on the words 1 John ii. 15, ‘if any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Mark viii. 34, ‘If any man would come after me, let him deny himself,’ etc. And on the words 1 John ii. 15, ‘if any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hard and grievous does that appear which the Lord hath enjoined, that “whosoever will come after Him, must deny himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what He enjoineth is not hard or grievous, who aideth us that what He enjoineth may be done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For both is that true which is said to Him in the Psalm, “Because of the words of Thy lips I have kept hard ways.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that is true which He said Himself, “My yoke is easy, and My burden is light.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whatsoever is hard in what is enjoined us, charity makes easy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We know what great things love itself can do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Very often is this love even abominable and impure; but how great hardships have men suffered, what indignities and intolerable things have they endured, to attain to the object of their love? whether it be a lover of money who is called covetous; or a lover of honour, who is called ambitious; or a lover of beautiful women, who is called voluptuous.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who could enumerate all sorts of loves?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet consider what labour all lovers undergo, and are not conscious of their labours; and then does any such one most feel labour, when he is hindered from labour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Since then the majority of men are such as their loves are, and that there ought to be no other care for the regulation of our lives, than the choice of that which we ought to love; why dost thou wonder, if he who loves Christ, and who wishes to follow Christ, for the love of Him denies himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if by loving himself man is lost, surely by denying himself he is found.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The first destruction of man, was the love of himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if he had not loved himself, if he had preferred God to himself, he would have been willing to be ever subject unto God; and would not have been turned to the neglect of His will, and the doing his own will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this is to love one’s self, to wish to do one’s own will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Prefer to this God’s will; learn to love thyself by not loving thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that ye may know that it is a vice to love one’s self, the Apostle speaks thus, “For men shall be lovers of their own selves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And can he who loves himself have any sure trust in himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No; for he begins to love himself by forsaking God, and is driven away from himself to love those things which are beyond himself; to such a degree that when the aforesaid Apostle had said,” Men shall be lovers of their own selves,” he subjoined immediately, “lovers of money.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Already thou seest that thou art without.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast begun to love thyself: stand in thyself if thou canst.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why goest thou without?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou, as being rich in money, become a lover of money?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast begun to love what is without thee, thou hast lost thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When a man’s love then goes even away from himself to those things which are without, he begins to share the vanity of his vain desires, and prodigal as it were to spend his strength.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is dissipated, exhausted, without resource or strength, he feeds swine; and wearied with this office of feeding swine, he at last remembers what he was, and says, “How many hired servants of my Father’s are eating bread, and I here perish with hunger!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when the son in the parable says this, what is said of him, who had squandered all he had on harlots, who wished to have in his own power what was being well kept for him with his father; he wished to have it at his own disposal, he squandered all, he was reduced to indigence: what is said of him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And when he returned to himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If “he returned to himself,” he had gone away from himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because he had fallen from himself, had gone away from himself, he returns first to himself, that he may return to that state from which he had fallen away by falling from himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as by falling away from himself, he remained in himself; so by returning to himself, he ought not to remain in himself, lest he again go away from himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Returning then to himself, that he might not remain in himself, what did he say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I will arise and go to my Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, whence he had fallen away from himself, he had fallen away from his Father; he had fallen away from himself, he had gone away from himself to those things which are without.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He returns to himself, and goes to his Father, where he may keep himself in all security.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then he had gone away from himself, let him also in returning to himself, from whom he had gone away, that he may “go to his Father,” deny himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “deny himself”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him not trust in himself, let him feel that he is a man, and have respect to the words of the prophet, “Cursed is every one that putteth his hope in man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him withdraw himself from himself, but not towards things below.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him withdraw himself from himself, that he may cleave unto God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatever of good he has, let him commit to Him by whom he was made; whatever of evil he has, he has made it for himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The evil that is in him God made not; let him destroy what himself has done, who has been thereby undone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let him deny himself,” He saith, “and take up his cross, and follow Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And whither must the Lord be followed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whither He is gone, we know; but a very few days since we celebrated the solemn memorial of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He has risen again, and ascended into heaven; thither must He be followed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Undoubtedly we must not despair of it, because He hath Himself promised us, not because man can do anything.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Heaven was far away from us, before that our Head had gone into heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now why should we despair, if we are members of that Head?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thither then must He be followed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who would be unwilling to follow Him to such an abode?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Especially seeing that we are in so great travail on earth with fears and pains.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who would be unwilling to follow Christ thither, where is supreme felicity, supreme peace, perpetual security?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Good is it to follow Him thither: but we must see by what way we are to follow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord Jesus did not say the words we are engaged in, when He had now risen from the dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He had not yet suffered, He had still to come to the Cross, had to come to His dishonouring, to the outrages, the scourging, the thorns, the wounds, the mockeries, the insults, Death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Rough as it were is the way; it makes thee to be slow; thou hast no mind to follow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But follow on.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Rough is the way which man has made for himself, but what Christ hath trodden in His passage is worn smooth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For who would not wish to go to exaltation?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Elevation is pleasing to all; but humility is the step to it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou put out thy foot beyond thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast a mind to fall, not to ascend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Begin by the step, and so thou hast ascended.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This step of humility those two disciples were loth to have an eye to, who said, “Lord, bid that one of us may sit at Thy right hand, and the other at the left in Thy kingdom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They sought for exaltation, they did not see the step.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Lord showed them the step.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what did He answer them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Ye who seek the hill of exaltation, can ye drink the cup of humiliation?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore He does not say simply, “Let him deny himself, and follow Me” howsoever: but He said more, “Let him take up his cross, and follow Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “Let him take up his cross”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him bear whatever trouble he has; so let him follow Me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when he shall begin to follow Me in conformity to My life and precepts, he will have many to contradict him, he will have many to hinder him, he will have many to dissuade him, and that from among those who are even as it were Christ’s companions.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who hindered the blind men from crying out were walking with Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whether therefore they be threats or caresses, or whatsoever hindrances there be, if thou wish to follow, turn them into thy cross, bear it, carry it, do not give way beneath it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There seems to be an exhortation to martyrdom in these words of the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If there be persecution, ought not all things to be despised in consideration of Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The world is loved; but let Him be preferred by whom the world was made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Great is the world; but greater is He by whom the world was made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fair is the world; but fairer is He by whom the world was made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Sweet is the world; but sweeter is He by whom the world was made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Evil is the world; and good is He by whom the world was made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How shall I be able to explain and unravel what I have said?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May God help me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what have I said? what have ye applauded?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, it is but a question, and yet ye have already applauded.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How is the world evil, if He by whom the world was made is good?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did not God make all things, “and behold they were very good”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does not Scripture at each several work of creation testify that God made it good, by saying, “And God saw that it was good,” and at the end summed them all up together thus how that God had made them, “And behold they were very good”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then is the world evil, and He good by whom the world was made?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Since the world was made by Him, and the world knew Him not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The world was made by Him, the heaven and earth and all things that are in them: “the world knew Him not,” the lovers of the world; the lovers of the world and the despisers of God; this “world knew Him not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the world is evil, because they are evil who prefer the world to God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He is good who made the world, the heaven, and earth, and sea, and themselves who love the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this only, that they love the world and do not love God, He made not in them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But themselves, all that appertains to their nature He made; what appertains to guiltiness, He made not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is that I said a little while ago, “Let man efface what he has made, and so will he be well-pleasing to Him who made Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there is among men themselves a good world also; but one that has been made good from being evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the whole world if you take the word “world” for men, putting aside (what we call the world) the heaven and earth and all things that in them are; if you take the world for men, the whole world did he who first sinned make evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The whole mass was corrupted in the root.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God made man good; so runs the Scripture, “God made man upright; and men themselves found out many cogitations.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Run from these “many” to One, gather up thy scattered things into one: flow on together, fence thyself in, abide with One; go not to many things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is blessedness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we have flowed away, have gone on to perdition: we were all born with sin, and to that sin wherein we were born have we too added by our evil living, and the whole world has become evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But Christ came, and He chose that which He made, not what He found; for He found all evil, and by His grace He made them good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so was made another “world;” and the “world” now persecutes the “world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the “world” which persecutes?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That of which it is said to us, “Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all that is in the world, is the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, which is not of the Father, but of the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the world passeth away, and the lust thereof: but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever,” even as God abideth for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have spoken of two “worlds,” the “world” which persecutes, and that which it persecutes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the “world” which persecutes?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “All that is the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, which is not of the Father, but of the world;” and “the world passeth away.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, this is the “world” which persecutes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the “world” which it persecutes?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He that doeth the will of God abideth for ever,” even as God abideth for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But see, that which persecutes is called the “world;” let us prove whether that also which suffers persecution is called “the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Art thou deaf to the voice of Christ who speaketh, or rather to Holy Scripture which testifieth, “God was in Christ reconciling the world unto Himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If the world hate you, know ye that it first hated Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, the “world” hates.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does it hate but the “world”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What “world”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “God was in Christ reconciling the world unto Himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The condemned “world” persecutes; the reconciled “world” suffers persecution.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The condemned “world” is all that is without the Church; the reconciled “world” is the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He says, “The Son of Man came not to judge the world, but that the world through Him may be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now in this world, holy, good, reconciled, saved, or rather to be saved, and now saved in hope, “for we are saved in hope;” in this world, I say, that is in the Church which wholly follows Christ, He hath said as of universal application, “Whosoever will follow Me, let him deny himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not that the virgins ought to give ear to this, and the married women ought not; or that the widows ought, and the women who still have their husbands ought not; or that monks ought, and the married men ought not; or that the clergy ought, and the laymen ought not: but let the whole Church, the whole body, all the members, distinguished and distributed throughout their several offices, follow Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the whole Church follow Him, that only Church, let the dove follow Him, let the spouse follow Him, let her who has been redeemed and endowed with the Bridegroom’s blood, follow Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There virgin purity hath its place; there widowed continence hath its place; married chastity there hath its place; but adultery hath no place of its own there; and no place there hath lasciviousness, unlawful and meet for punishment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let these several members which have their place there, in their kind and place and measure, “follow Christ;” let them “deny themselves;” that is, let them presume nothing of themselves: let them “take up their cross,” that is, let them in the world endure for Christ’s sake whatever the world may bring upon them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them love Him, who Alone doth not deceive, who Alone is not deceived, Alone deceiveth not; let them love Him, for that is true which He doth promise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because He doth not give at once, faith wavers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hold on, persevere, endure, bear delay and thou hast borne the cross.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not the virgin say, “I shall alone be there.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Mary shall not be there alone but the widow Anna shall be there also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not the woman which hath an husband say, “The widow will be there, not I;” for it is not that Anna will be there, and Susanna not be there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But by all means let them who would be there prove themselves hereby, that they who have here a lower place envy not, but love in others the better place.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, for instance, my Brethren, that ye may understand me; one man has chosen a married life, another a life of continence; if he who has chosen the married life, has adulterous lusts, he has “looked back;” he has lusted after that which is unlawful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He too who would wish afterwards to return from continence to a married life, has “looked back;” he has chosen what is in itself lawful, yet he has “looked back.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is marriage then to be condemned?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No. Marriage is not to be condemned; but see whither he had come who has chosen it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He had already got before it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When he was living as a young man in voluptuousness, marriage was before him; he was making his way towards it; but when he had chosen continence, marriage was behind him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Remember,” saith the Lord, “Lot’s wife.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lot’s wife, by looking behind, remained motionless.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whatever point then any one has been able to reach, let him fear to “look back” from thence; and let him walk in the way, let him “follow Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Forgetting those things which are behind, and stretching forth unto those things which are before, let him by an earnest inward intention press on toward the prize of the calling of God in Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let those that are married regard the unmarried as above themselves; let them acknowledge that they are better; let them in them love what themselves have not; and let them in them love Christ.
